paper_title: Towards interactive reinforcement learning with intrinsic feedback

challenges_discussed.definition: Challenges for simply being able to learn

subcategories.name: robustness
subcategories.description: Ability of an agent to perform a task and generalize within/between environments of similar complexity

subcategories.name: scalability
subcategories.description: Ability of an agent to scale up to more complex environments

subcategories.name: aptness
subcategories.description: Rate at which an agent can learn a desired behavior

challenges_discussed.definition: Challenges concerned with learning as intended (i.e., learning to behave as a human dictates)
challenges_discussed.example: Paperclip Maximizer - an agent that maximizes its goal of making paperclips by turning the world into a paperclip factory
challenges_discussed.note: RL algorithms often display lesser forms of misalignment

brain_computer_interfaces.definition: Systems for monitoring the brain and utilizing neural activity as a direct means of communication between the brain and external devices

rl_bci_integration.description: Integration of RL and BCI where particular neural activity is interpreted as a reward in the RL framework
rl_bci_integration.note: Can be more broadly formulated using interactive RL concepts

interactive_rl_concerns.primary_concern: Seamlessly integrating and effectively communicating human intentions to an agent

human_input_channels.description: One of two main channels of communication
One of two main channels of communication

feedback_types.description: Human communicating directly with the agent via a button push or very basic natural language

results_section.limitations: Both explicit and implicit feedback do not cover the entire range of potential human feedback mediums

title: Towards interactive reinforcement learning with intrinsic feedback
